steps.title: Express a 7% increase algebraically
steps.content:
An increase of 7% adds 0.07 times the original amount to the original:

$$\text{2016 population} = \text{2015 population} + 0.07 \cdot \text{2015 population}$$

Factor the right side:

$$\text{2016 population} = (1 + 0.07) \cdot \text{2015 population} = 1.07 \cdot \text{2015 population}$$
steps.formula: $$\text{New} = (1 + p) \cdot \text{Original}, \text{ where } p = \text{decimal form of percentage}$$

steps.title: Identify the constant k
steps.content: The problem states that the 2016 population is $k$ times the 2015 population. From our factorization, this multiplier is \$1.07$, so $k = 1.07$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
